data.title: Fortescue's March-Quarter Iron Ore Shipments, Costs Rise
data.description:
(22:30 GMT) *Fortescue Approves US$680M Pilbara Green Energy Expansion
(22:30 GMT) *Fortescue: Investment Is Beyond US$6.2B Decarbonization Program
(22:31 GMT) *Fortescue Plans Off-Grid Renewable Energy System, Battery Storage and Firming
(22:31 GMT) *Fortescue Expects to Complete Project by 2028
(22:32 GMT) *Fortescue Sees Pathway to Multi-Gigawatt Expansion Beyond 2030
(22:33 GMT) *Fortescue 3Q Iron Ore Shipments 48.4 Million Tons, Up 5% On-Year
(22:35 GMT) *Fortescue 3Q Hematite C1 Unit Cost $18.29/Wet Ton, Up 4% On-Year
(22:36 GMT) *Fortescue 3Q Iron Ore Shipments 47.8 Million Tons (Fortescue Share), Up 5% On-Year
(22:36 GMT) *Fortescue 3Q Iron Bridge Concentrate Shipments 2.0 Million Tons
(22:37 GMT) *Fortescue 3Q Hematite Average Revenue $92/Ton; 89% of Average Platts 61% Index
(22:39 GMT) *Fortescue 3Q Iron Bridge Concentrate Revenue $122/Ton; 101% of Average Platts 65% Index
(22:39 GMT) *Fortescue Net Debt $1.6 Billion at March 31
(22:39 GMT) *Fortescue Cash Balance $4.2 Billion at March 31
(22:40 GMT) *Fortescue Reiterates FY26 Total Shipment, C1 Unit Cost, Capex Guidance
(22:41 GMT) *Fortescue: Portfolio Optimization of Pilbara Operations Is Underway
(22:41 GMT) *Fortescue Considering Port Outload Capacity and Maximizing Portfolio Value
(22:42 GMT) *Fortescue Expects to Update Market in Three Months
(22:59 GMT) Fortescue's March-Quarter Iron Ore Shipments, Costs Rise

By Rhiannon Hoyle

Fortescue reported a 5% rise in third-quarter iron ore shipments year over year, although production costs at its Australian hematite mining operations also rose.

Fortescue, the world's fourth-biggest iron-ore producer, on Friday said it shipped 48.4 million metric tons of the steelmaking ingredient in the three months through March, up from 46.1 million tons a year earlier.

Shipments were down 4% on the prior quarter, weighed by adverse weather conditions including two tropical cyclones.

Analysts expected quarterly shipments of roughly 48.7 million tons, according to a consensus estimate compiled by Visible Alpha.

The miner reported a so-called C1 cost for its hematite mines of $18.29 a wet ton. That was down 4% on the December quarter, but 4% higher than a year earlier.

Fortescue reiterated its annual guidance for total shipments, costs and capital expenditure for the 12 months through June. However, it lowered its shipment forecast from its Iron Bridge operations to 9 million-10 million tons, from 10 million-12 million previously.

The ASX-listed operator said it would spend $680 million on an off-grid renewable energy system and large-scale battery storage in the iron ore-rich Pilbara region of Western Australia state.

Fortescue said the project, which it expects to be complete by 2028, is in addition to its existing $6.2 billion decarbonization plan.

Write to Rhiannon Hoyle at rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com

data.title: MPH: Revenue up 32% to $28.9M, but net loss widened to $7.1M on higher costs and CMS rebate
data.description:
Net revenue grew 32% year-over-year to $28.9 million, driven by pharmacy acquisitions, but net loss widened to $7.1 million due to a CMS rebate, higher R&D, and amortization. Pharmacy segment revenue doubled, while AGGRASTAT® sales declined amid generic competition.

Original document: Medicure Inc. [MPH] Earnings Release — Apr. 27 2026
